items.title: 【グッチ】日本限定&先行アイテムも！ スーパーラッキーデーに向けたスモールレザーグッズが登場
items.summary: Gucci released Japan-exclusive small leather goods timed to July 19's triple-auspicious "Super Lucky Day." The GG Marmont line comes in pastel pink/blue wallets and card cases, while the Gucci Lettering line pouches and wallets debut in Japan ahead of worldwide release. Coverage frames the drop around Japanese gift-giving/fortune customs rather than runway news.

items.title: 最強開運日に手に入れたい。「グッチ」の日本限定ウォレット＆レザーグッズ
items.summary: Lifestyle-media pickup of Gucci's Japan-only wallet and leather-goods capsule for the July 19 "strongest luck day," including the Ando Shippoten (cloisonne) capsule collaboration referencing traditional shippo-yaki enamel craft. Framed as a shopping guide rather than brand analysis; illustrates continued reliance on limited-edition drops to drive Japan store traffic.

items.source: Sina Finance (新浪财经, via 知乎-syndicated Q&A feature)
items.title: 古驰这两款入门包二手保值率大概多少？
items.summary: Chinese resale-market explainer states Gucci's classic canvas bags hold 55-65% of original retail value secondhand versus Louis Vuitton's typical 50-70%, but warns Gucci's seasonal/non-classic styles depreciate over 85% once trend cycles end (often just 1-2 seasons), making Gucci a weaker store-of-value than LV in the Chinese resale market.

items.title: 구찌는 왜 브랜드가 휘청이게 되었을까에 대해
items.summary: Active Korean community thread (originally posted 2025, still drawing fresh comments this month) diagnosing Gucci's decline: commenters cite counterfeit proliferation, loss of exclusivity from mass adoption, repeated price hikes without design payoff, and 'streetwear/hoodlum' brand image damage. Broad agreement in replies rather than defensive pushback, reflecting sustained negative community sentiment toward the brand.

items.title: GUCCI/古驰_抖音品牌数据分析
items.summary: Douyin (TikTok China) commerce-analytics dashboard shows Gucci's trailing 30-day livestream/short-video GMV at roughly RMB 63.7M across ~281,000 units sold, average order value RMB 204-250, with livestreams (60% of sales, ~1,933 sessions, 68 affiliated creators) dominating over short-video posts — indicating Gucci's Chinese social-commerce activity skews toward discount-adjacent live-selling rather than full-price brand storytelling.
